page.title: Accounts
page.subtitle: Manage your bank accounts, credit cards, and investments

list.empty: No accounts found. Create your first account to get started!

actions.closeTitleDisabled: Account must have zero balance to close

confirmDialog.closeMessage: Are you sure you want to close "{name}"? The account must have a zero balance to be closed.
confirmDialog.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to permanently delete "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

form.currencyLocked: Currency is locked because this account already has transactions. Changing it would silently re-denominate existing balances.
form.noScheduledPayments: This account does not have scheduled payments configured.
form.statementSettlementDayTooltip: The settlement date (also called the closing date) is the last day of the billing cycle. Transactions posted on or before this day will appear on the current statement.
form.investmentPairDescription: Creates two linked accounts: a Cash account for transfers in/out and a Brokerage account for investment transactions. This is the recommended structure for tracking investments.

exportModal.title: Export {name}
exportModal.customFormatHelp: Use Y for year, M for month, D for day with separators (- / ')
exportModal.customDateFormatEmpty: Please enter a custom date format

loanPaymentSetup.extraPayments: {count, plural, one {# extra principal payment} other {# extra principal payments}} detected (avg {currency}{avg}/period)
loanPaymentSetup.reviewValues: Review and adjust the values below before saving.
loanPaymentSetup.useDetectedSplit: Use principal/interest split from imported transactions
loanPaymentSetup.useDetectedSplitDesc: Will use {currency}{interest} for interest, remainder to principal
loanPaymentSetup.calculateFromRate: Will calculate split from the interest rate and current balance
loanPaymentSetup.fillRequiredFields: Please fill in all required fields
loanPaymentSetup.requiredFieldsMissing: Please fill in all required fields

mortgageFields.title: Mortgage Details
mortgageFields.canadianMortgageDesc: Uses semi-annual compounding for fixed rates (required by law in Canada)
mortgageFields.variableRateDesc: Rate may change during the term (uses monthly compounding)
mortgageFields.termLengthNoTerm: Leave at 0 years and 0 months for no term.

interestBookingMode.label: How is interest recorded?
interestBookingMode.help: Lets rate detection read your interest correctly. Auto works for most setups.

bookingHelp.title: How to record loan payments
bookingHelp.intro: Each installment has two parts that mean different things in your finances, so record them separately:
bookingHelp.principalBody: It repays borrowed money, not a cost. Record it as a transfer from your bank account to the loan account and leave it uncategorized, so it lowers the loan balance without showing up in expenses or category breakdowns.
bookingHelp.interestBody: It is the real cost of the loan. Record it as an expense with a category (for example "Loans").
bookingHelp.avoid: Avoid: don't record the whole installment as a single expense. It overstates your costs (the real cost is only the interest) and the debt on the loan account never goes down.
bookingHelp.overpayments: Overpayments work like principal: a transfer to the loan account, uncategorized. If the bank added interest to an overpayment, split that part out as a separate expense.

overpaymentRecognition.title: Overpayment recognition
overpaymentRecognition.description: How the schedule tells a standalone overpayment (100% principal) apart from a regular installment. A payment matching any one of these counts as an overpayment.
overpaymentRecognition.memoHelp: Case-insensitive substring match against the payment's memo, its source transaction's memo, or a split memo.

assetFields.title: Asset Value Change Settings
assetFields.description: Select a category that will be used to track value changes for this asset (e.g., "Home Value Change", "Vehicle Depreciation").
assetFields.dateAcquiredNote: The asset will be excluded from net worth calculations before this date.

validation.institutionRequired: Please select or create an institution

schedule.title: Loan Schedule
schedule.subtitle: {historical, plural, one {# payment made} other {# payments made}}, {projected, plural, one {# projected} other {# projected}}
schedule.toggleMonth: Show or hide the payments made in {month}

rateHistory.title: Rate History
rateHistory.description: Track interest-rate changes over the life of the loan, from variable-rate resets to term renewals.
rateHistory.empty: No rate changes recorded. Add one when your rate changes, or detect past changes from your payment history.
rateHistory.paymentModeRecalculate: Recalculate payment to keep the amortization on track
rateHistory.deleteMessage: Delete the rate change effective {date}? This cannot be undone.
rateHistory.detectMessage: This analyzes your payment history and replaces previously detected entries. Entries you added or edited yourself are kept.
rateHistory.detectedToast: {count, plural, =0 {No new rate changes detected} one {# rate change detected} other {# rate changes detected}}
rateHistory.detectFailed: Could not detect rate changes from the payment history
rateHistory.scheduledUpdateMessage: The scheduled bill payment "{name}" can be updated to match the new rate: {payment} total, split into {principal} principal and {interest} interest. Update it now?
rateHistory.scheduledUpdateFailed: Could not update the scheduled payment

interestCategory.title: Interest category
interestCategory.description: Where this loan's interest is recorded. When interest is booked as a separate transaction rather than a split of the payment, naming its category lets the schedule show real interest and lets "Detect from history" find enough interest to work.
interestCategory.label: Interest category
interestCategory.saveError: Failed to save the interest category

overpaymentCategory.title: Overpayment Detection
overpaymentCategory.description: Recognize standalone overpayments by category, by memo text, or both. Either match counts a payment as 100% principal, so the schedule flags it instead of treating it as a regular installment.
overpaymentCategory.label: Overpayment category
overpaymentCategory.memoHelp: Payments whose memo contains this text count as overpayments (case-insensitive).
overpaymentCategory.saveError: Failed to save the overpayment settings

simulator.title: Overpayment Simulator
simulator.description: Model extra payments to see how much sooner the loan could be paid off and how much interest you would save. Simulations do not change your real payments.
simulator.modeShortenTermHint: Keep the same payment and pay the loan off sooner. Maximum interest saved.
simulator.modeLowerInstallmentHint: Keep the same payoff date and recalculate a smaller payment on the reduced balance.
simulator.detectedExtraHint: Your payment history shows an average of {amount} in extra principal per payment.

chart.title: Payoff Timeline
chart.empty: No payment history or projection available.
chart.scenarioNote: The overpayment scenario is a simulation and does not change your scheduled payments.

scenarios.title: Saved Scenarios
scenarios.empty: No saved scenarios yet. Set up an overpayment above and save it to compare plans over time.
scenarios.lumpSumSummary: {count, plural, one {# lump sum} other {# lump sums}}
scenarios.deleteMessage: Delete the scenario "{name}"? This cannot be undone.

pastImpact.title: Impact of Overpayments Made
pastImpact.description: How the payments you have already made compare against the original contractual schedule.
pastImpact.extraPrincipalNote: Total extra principal paid on top of your scheduled payments
pastImpact.missingData: To see how your overpayments have shortened this loan, this account needs an interest rate, a payment frequency, and some recorded payments to reconstruct its original schedule.
